description:
You are acting as a junior backup-operator who must validate that a recent restore-test
    covered every critical file often requested by customers.  
    Two comma-separated log files produced by the restore appliance have already been copied
    to the working directory:

        /home/user/restore_tests/raw_logs/log_day1.csv
        /home/user/restore_tests/raw_logs/log_day2.csv

    1. File layout of every CSV line  
       Each line has exactly three comma-separated fields in this order:

           ISO-8601-UTC-timestamp , absolute_file_path , textual_status

       Example:  
           2023-09-01T02:13:22Z,/etc/nginx/nginx.conf,RESTORED

    2. Your goal  
       a. Merge the two logs.  
       b. Count how many total lines mention each distinct absolute_file_path
          (status does NOT matter – count every occurrence).  
       c. Produce a *frequency report* sorted by:
            • primary key: descending numeric count,  
            • secondary key: ascending alphabetical order of the file path  
          (so files with the same count are listed alphabetically).  

    3. Exact output that the automated test will look for  
       • Create directory (if missing):  
             /home/user/restore_tests/output  

       • Inside it create plain text file  
             /home/user/restore_tests/output/restore_frequency_report.txt  

       • The file **must** contain one line per unique file path, following
         this strict, tab-separated layout (TAB characters, not spaces):

             <rank starting at 1> <TAB> <total_count> <TAB> <absolute_file_path><NEWLINE>

         Rank is simply the 1-based ordinal position after the required sort.
         A final newline is required after the last line.

         Example of the required structure (values here are illustrative only):

             1<TAB>42<TAB>/path/example1<NEWLINE>
             2<TAB>13<TAB>/another/path<NEWLINE>

    4. Auditing your work (important for future investigations)  
       • Create an additional file  
             /home/user/restore_tests/output/command_history.log  
       • Append every shell command you actually used—exactly as typed—one per line.
         There is no particular format beyond “one command, one line”.  
         The test-suite only verifies that this file exists and is not empty, so do
         not leave it blank.

    5. Restrictions & hints  
       • DO NOT rename, move, or alter the original CSV files.  
       • You are encouraged to solve the task entirely with standard GNU/Linux
         command-line utilities such as cut, sort, uniq, awk, etc.  
       • You do not have root privileges; everything happens under /home/user.  
       • Only the content of restore_frequency_report.txt will be numerically checked,
         so make sure tab characters and sort order are exact.

    When you are done, both files must exist with the exact paths and contents
    described above.
truth:
<!--  Contents of the pre-created raw log files  -->
    <!--  /home/user/restore_tests/raw_logs/log_day1.csv -->
    2023-09-01T02:13:22Z,/etc/nginx/nginx.conf,RESTORED
    2023-09-01T02:14:01Z,/var/www/html/index.html,RESTORED
    2023-09-01T02:14:45Z,/etc/nginx/nginx.conf,RESTORED
    2023-09-01T02:15:10Z,/var/www/html/index.html,FAILURE
    2023-09-01T02:15:55Z,/home/user/.bashrc,RESTORED
    2023-09-01T02:16:27Z,/etc/nginx/nginx.conf,RESTORED
    2023-09-01T02:17:08Z,/var/www/html/main.css,RESTORED

    <!--  /home/user/restore_tests/raw_logs/log_day2.csv -->
    2023-09-02T11:05:00Z,/etc/nginx/nginx.conf,RESTORED
    2023-09-02T11:06:14Z,/var/www/html/index.html,RESTORED
    2023-09-02T11:06:52Z,/home/user/.bashrc,RESTORED
    2023-09-02T11:07:33Z,/var/www/html/main.css,RESTORED
    2023-09-02T11:08:17Z,/etc/nginx/nginx.conf,RESTORED
    2023-09-02T11:08:59Z,/home/user/.bashrc,FAILURE
    2023-09-02T11:09:41Z,/var/www/html/index.html,RESTORED

    <!--  Expected end-state file created by the agent  -->
    <!--  /home/user/restore_tests/output/restore_frequency_report.txt -->
    1\t5\t/etc/nginx/nginx.conf\n
    2\t4\t/var/www/html/index.html\n
    3\t3\t/home/user/.bashrc\n
    4\t2\t/var/www/html/main.css\n

    # A newline character (\n) follows every line, including the last.
    # The command_history.log file must exist and be non-empty.  Its exact
    # contents are not evaluated by the automated test, only existence and size.
name: task_000000_98dcfc06
